english: adv. happening very quickly in an unexpected way
example: Suddenly, the door opened and Dad, Mom, and Michael jumped into her room.

english: adj. unable to understand or think clearly
example: Emma sat up in her bed and looked confused.

english: adv.on or to a lower floor of a building
n. a large piece of paper with writing or a picture on it.
example: She saw all the banners, balloons, and ribbons.
She slowly realized that today was her birthday, not yesterday!

english: n. something that is not correct
v. to spoil or destroy something
example: "A mistake that ruined my whole day," Emma thought.

english: to make a picture in your mind
example: Emma started to imagine a beautiful party with nice snacks and lots of games to play.

english: n. something that is added to make it more attractive
example: She imagined beautiful decorations and all her friends.

english: n. a belief that something will happen
example: She learned not to have too many expectations.
